_comment: Device registry — source of truth for device -> antenna -> filter -> band -> domain -> endpoint. Replaces the RF switch. The pi-acquisition provisioner iterates `present: true` devices only; flip present + re-apply to join a new tuner. See docs/acquisition_tier.md and docs/PLATFORM_V2_ARCHITECTURE.md.

_antenna_ports._comment: RSPdx-R2 is a single tuner with 3 software-selectable antenna inputs (one band at a time). 'antenna'/'feed'/'filter' above describe the ACTIVE port (A, the FM job). Port B now carries an AM loop -> 9:1 balun (added 2026-06-17, reusing the port the dipole + Sawbird+ NOAA LNA vacated 2026-06-16). Port C still carries the AM long-wire, consumed by radio-compute's am_stream.py over the remote dx-R2. NOTE: dx-R2 is single-tuner/one-band-at-a-time and Antenna A owns it for live FM, so B/C remain physical-readiness only until AM is sampled (on-demand preempt, or moved to the HF+ YouLoop).

hf-plus._comment: Airspy HF+ — attached 2026-06-16, served on the Pi (sdr-source@hf-plus :55002, SoapyAirspyHF built by the pi provisioner). YouLoop attached 2026-06-17 (replaced the interim whip). NOTE: the YouLoop is a passive HF/MW magnetic loop — great for the HF+'s am-broadcast role, but it is NOT a VHF antenna, so it likely degrades the NOAA Weather Radio reception (162.550 MHz) that wx-stream.py serves on /wx.mp3 off the whip's ~60 dB. Re-verify /wx.mp3 + the NWR scan after the swap. present:true makes the pi-acquisition provisioner lay down its source server; the radio-compute AM consumer still reads the dx-R2 long-wire (Antenna C) — moving AM onto the HF+ YouLoop is a deliberate radio-compute follow-up (touches the live FM stream), not yet done.

rtl-2838._comment: RETIRED 2026-06-16 — the Nooelec RTL2838 was physically removed (taken to the attic for troubleshooting on a separate Pi). Its discone moved to the Airspy R2. Scanner P25 cut over to airspy-r2 in the same change (present:false here, airspy-r2 present:true). Kept in the registry for history/rollback; the device is not plugged in.

airspy-r2._comment: LIVE scanner source as of 2026-06-16 — carries the discone (moved off the retired rtl-2838). Gain elements are LNA/MIX/VGA (0-15 each), NOT the R820T 'TUNER' element — op25 run-op25.sh GAINS is hand-set on .83 accordingly. Native CS16, rates 2.5/10 MSps (held at 2.5).

rtl-v4._comment: ON ORDER (2026-06-19) to REPLACE the flaky nooelec-wx for the Meteor-LRPT role on p24. The Nooelec NESDR proved unreliable (drops off the bus across hosts incl. the attic), so an RTL v4 was ordered as a known-good dongle. When it arrives: put it on p24 with the Meteor V-dipole (+Sawbird), give it the rtl_tcp acquisition role the nooelec-wx entry held (or fold those settings here), flip present:true, re-apply pi_wxsat + radio_compute. Feed/filter below describe the intended setup, not what is plugged in.

nooelec-wx._comment: SHELVED 2026-06-19 (present:false). Nooelec NESDR SMArt v5 (RTL2838, Meteor V-dipole) on the OUTDOOR ADS-B Pi p24.srvr (192.168.6.141). PAUSED because the NOOELEC DONGLE ITSELF IS FLAKY (per the user: same failures in the attic on a different host — it drops off the bus / -4/-1/-71; an RTL v4 is ON ORDER to replace it). NOT a p24-USB-marginal problem: p24's two ADS-B dongles run for weeks, and this Nooelec failed in every config (3-dongle, solo, blue USB-3). It wedged overnight 2026-06-18/19 (rtl_tcp ConnectionReset -> all captures got no IQ). The afternoon passes that DID record (clean, gain 7.2dB/250k) decoded NOSYNC = reception unresolved. UN-SHELVE when the RTL v4 arrives: swap it in (update serial/device), present:true, terraform apply pi_wxsat + radio_compute, re-enable wxsat-rtltcp (p24) + wxsat-scheduler (.84). User unplugged the Nooelec + re-plugged both ADS-B dongles 2026-06-19; p24 is back to its ADS-B feeder role. The whole acquire->decode->GUI pipeline is proven. See memory wxsat-meteor-on-p24.

goes._comment: GOES-19 HRIT/EMWIN geostationary downlink on goes.srvr (a dedicated Pi 5): Nooelec SmArTee RTL-SDR + Sawbird GOES LNA + dish. UNLIKE every other device, GOES is geostationary/continuous, so SatDump runs `live goes_hrit` ON THE PI (decode does NOT move to the rack). The Pi's goes.service command (gain/freq/samplerate) is hand-tuned and KEPT-IF-ABSENT by the pi-goes provisioner — do not auto-rewrite it from this registry (these values are documentary defaults used only on a fresh install). The rack LXC goes-archive (903/.85) rsync-PULLS goes_output and serves the gallery + goes.rg2.io API + the weather2 headline image. domain:goes keeps it out of the radio-compute device loops.

adsb-1090._comment: 1090ES ADS-B on p24.srvr (RTL2832U serial 00001090). p24 DECODES with readsb and serves Beast/SBS on the LAN; the rack adsb-feeder LXC (904/.86) runs ultrafeeder which ingests + aggregates + feeds FA/FR24/ADSBx + MLAT + tar1090 (adsb.rg2.io). domain:adsb keeps it out of the other compute loops. SELECT BY SERIAL, never index — on p24 the enumeration order is REVERSED (index 0 = the 978 dongle).

adsb-978._comment: 978 UAT ADS-B on p24.srvr (RTL2838 serial 00000001) via dump978-fa (kept as-is). raw out :30978; the rack ultrafeeder ingests it as uat_in. Same p24->rack hub flow as adsb-1090.

davis-vantage._comment: Davis Vantage weather station console on weather2.srvr (Pi Zero 2 W). NOT an SDR — a serial weather station read over Bluetooth rfcomm (/dev/rfcomm0, console MAC 00:1B:DC:50:05:DE). weewx COLLECTION stays on the Zero (the Vantage DMPAFT archive download only works over the local BT serial — it failed `no <ACK>` over a ser2net/TCP relay, so the old bridge model was abandoned). The Zero keeps collecting + uploading (WU/PWSweather/Influx/MQTT) and replicates its archive DB to the rack weather-compute LXC (905/.87) via Litestream (SFTP push). The rack restores the replica + runs report-only weewx (Belchertown + Seasons) + nginx, and serves bobgardner.org. domain:weather keeps it out of the SDR loops. The pi-weather module has a `cutover` flag (default false = install Litestream idle, change nothing live).
